panel_fork_updates_hint: Arguments you voted on have been forked.
panel_budget_hint: Votes and new theses. Resets daily.
home_list_empty: No theses yet. Be the first to create one!
home_list_limit_note: Adjust the complexity slider to see more theses.
error_too_many_requests: Too many requests — wait a moment and try again.
error_thesis_limit_reached: Daily thesis limit reached (7/day). Try again tomorrow.
error_text_too_long: Text too long — please shorten it.
danger_zone_hint: Actions that change the thesis's visibility. Reversible, but visible to everyone.
danger_zone_archive_desc: Hides it from listings and stops new arguments and votes. Existing content stays readable.
danger_zone_unarchive_desc: Brings it back into listings. Arguments and votes reopen.
argform_fork_hint: You are creating a new argument based on an existing one. Both will exist in parallel.
argform_content_hint: Links are auto-detected. Just paste URLs into your text.
argform_content_placeholder: State your reasoning. Paste sources as URLs — they will be classified automatically.
argform_emotional_hint: (Herzensangelegenheit — a heart matter. Overrides evidence detection.)
vote_support_hint: Support (click again for weight up to 3)
vote_reject_hint: Reject (click again for weight up to 3)
argcol_add_disabled_support: Daily budget for support arguments depleted
argcol_add_disabled_reject: Daily budget for reject arguments depleted
argpool_hint: Sorted by community support and freshness. Enough support and they rise into the top columns above.
argpool_empty: No further arguments — the top columns show everything so far.
my_page_subtitle: Theses you created or voted on.
my_empty_state: You haven't participated yet. Go vote or create a thesis!
my_standpoint_sub: Reflection report on your work so far — no blaming, just a reference.
my_budget_sub: What you've spent from today's budget (limit: {limit} per bucket).
complexity_slider_hint: Adjust the complexity slider to see more or less.
pulse_page_subtitle: What moves the community — observed, not judged.
about_lead: Quappe is an argumentation platform. Theses, arguments, evidence — no comment wars, no flame trails, no anonymous insults masked as opinion.
about_purpose_p1_html: Language is imprecise, opinions are everywhere — but commonality is hard to find. Social media has built bubbles, fronts and hate. Quappe is a different attempt: a structured space where positions can be stated clearly, supported with reasoning, challenged with rigour, and respected even where they differ.
about_purpose_p2_html: We search for shared ground. Where we disagree, we disagree with respect. Opinion without argumentation simply weighs less in the discourse — that is the design.
about_how_1_html: Theses are the central entity — a claim someone proposes.
about_how_2_html: Arguments support or reject a thesis. They can carry evidence.
about_how_3_html: Fork instead of reply. Disagree with an argument? Copy it, adapt it, and let the crowd vote.
about_how_4_html: Votes are free. Creating costs a small daily budget — to encourage thoughtfulness.
about_how_5_html: Complexity slider controls how much you see at once.
about_how_6_html: Updates feed (/my/updates) — see who forks your arguments, who reacts to your theses. Adjust your position when it matters.
about_lifecycle_intro: A thesis is never static. It goes through phases — driven by real activity, not by anyone's opinion of it. The state you see on each card tells you where a thesis stands in the discourse right now.
about_lc_seedling_desc: Fresh out of the ground. Fewer than 7 days old, gathering first reactions.
about_lc_discussed_desc: Healthy activity. People are engaging, but no clear direction yet.
about_lc_contested_desc: Active but polarised — support and reject are roughly balanced. The hard, interesting cases live here.
about_lc_crystallized_desc: A clear majority (≥ 65 %) backed by solid arguments. Something the community has worked through.
about_lc_faded_desc: Activity dropped without reaching consensus. Not deleted — still reachable, just quiet.
about_lc_dormant_desc: Untouched for 90+ days. Kept as a cold reference; can wake up when new activity arrives.
about_lc_note_html: This is intentional. The platform is designed to let noise fade and surface what crystallises — not everything deserves the same attention forever.
about_rule_1: The integrity of a being is untouchable. Always.
about_rule_2: Separate the person from the position. Always.
about_rule_3: Good faith. Assume it. Earn it.
about_rule_4: Minorities deserve a voice and a safe space.
about_rule_5: Trolls get marked — kindly first, firmly later.
about_rule_6: Qualify yourself. Bring reasoning, bring evidence, bring nuance.
about_not_p: Quappe is not a public square for the unwilling. It is not a fighting place — except inside the arguments themselves. It is not an exposure of the vulnerable. And it is not a substitute for thinking — it is a tool for it.
about_transparency_intro: A short honest note on what runs where — so you know what you're using.
about_transp_identity_html: Identity. Anonymous but server-verified. On first visit the server mints a random UUID, signs it with an HMAC secret, and stores it in an httpOnly cookie. Every request is checked against that signature — request bodies can't forge someone else's identity. No accounts, no email, no tracking cookies. Clear your cookies and you're a new person.
about_transp_abuse_html: Abuse guards. Server-side length caps (title 200, description 2000, argument 800 chars), a 32 KB body cap, and token-bucket rate limits per IP + user (10 writes/min, 30 votes/min, 60 reads/min). Only the author can edit or delete their own thesis or argument.
about_transp_data_html: Data storage. Everything lives in the Node process's memory for now. Restart the server and the state is gone (seed data reloads). Phase 2 will bring real persistence.
about_transp_search_html: Semantic search. Runs locally. The multilingual e5 embedding model (via @xenova/transformers) is downloaded on first server start and cached under .cache/. No embeddings ever leave your machine.
about_transp_llm_html: Reflection reports (/my, /pulse). Generated by a local LLM via Ollama, if you have it running. Your theses, arguments and votes are sent only to http://127.0.0.1:11434 on your own machine. User content is wrapped in delimiter tags with an explicit "this is data, not instructions" system prompt to reduce prompt-injection surface. If Ollama is not running, the pages fall back to raw stats — no third-party LLM is ever contacted.
about_transp_updates_html: Updates feed. /my/updates aggregates reactions to your content (forks, new arguments, votes) from the server-side data. The "last seen" timestamp lives in localStorage; the badge counts anything newer than that.
about_transp_budget_html: Daily budget. Enforced client-side, stored in localStorage. The Your day panel reconstructs today's actions from the server so you can see where the budget went.
about_transp_analytics_html: No analytics. No trackers, no third-party scripts, no fingerprinting. The only network traffic is between your browser and this Quappe server (and optionally your local Ollama).
about_license_p_html: The code is source-available under a non-commercial license (PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0).
about_license_no_1: Selling it or running it as a paid service
about_license_no_2: Commercial advantage inside a for-profit company
about_license_note_html: This is deliberately not OSI-approved open source. We want the code visible and improvable — but we do not want anyone monetising the same anti-social patterns we're trying to leave behind.
settings_id_hint: This is your unique identity. It's stored locally and never shared visibly.
settings_role_hint: In MVP mode, all users are admins.
settings_bounds_hint: Adjust the min and max limits of the slider. The slider itself stays 0-100 but is mapped to the range you configure here.
settings_categories_hint: Manage the available categories for theses. Add or remove as needed.
settings_admin_hint: Available in MVP for everyone. Will be role-gated later.
settings_about_hint: Quappe is an argumentation platform. State theses, provide arguments, and find commonality through structured discourse. No usernames. No comment wars. Just arguments.
top_limit_note: Adjust the complexity slider to see more theses.
updates_page_subtitle: How others react to your content — the basis for refining your arguments.
updates_empty_body: When others react to your theses or arguments, you'll see it here.
about_explore_intro: Each mechanic has a page of its own.
about_explore_lifecycle_desc: How a thesis moves from seedling to crystallized — or fades.
about_explore_heat_desc: What the coloured left band on a thesis means.
about_explore_voting_desc: Support / reject / neutral, the budget, and weighted votes.
about_explore_fork_desc: When and how to fork an argument someone else wrote.
about_explore_complexity_desc: The slider that decides how many items you see.
about_explore_transparency_desc: Identity, data storage, LLM use, license.
about_heat_lead: How much recent activity a thesis has — visible as a coloured band on the left of every thesis card.
about_heat_what_p_html: Heat compares recent activity (votes and arguments over the last few days) against the thesis's own long-run baseline. A ratio above 1 means the thesis is receiving more attention than usual; below 1 means it has cooled off.
about_heat_hot_desc: Ratio ≥ 2 — noticeably more activity than the baseline. Worth checking in.
about_heat_warm_desc: Ratio between 1 and 2 — steady discussion, above average.
about_heat_cool_desc: Ratio between 0.5 and 1 — some activity, below the thesis's own average.
about_heat_cold_desc: Ratio < 0.5 — quiet. The thesis may have exhausted the current interest.
about_heat_why_p: Heat is a signal, not a ranking. A cold thesis can still be well-argued and settled; a hot thesis can be a fresh flare-up. Use heat to decide where your attention makes the most difference right now.
about_voting_lead: Three vote kinds, one budget, and a weighted option for the votes you care about most.
about_voting_support_desc: You agree with this thesis or argument.
about_voting_neutral_desc: You've read it and considered it, but neither support nor reject captures your view.
about_voting_budget_p_html: Every user starts each cycle with a fixed number of votes. When you spend them, the counter goes down. This forces prioritisation — you can't drown out every conversation. The current budget is shown in the left sidebar; click to expand for details.
about_voting_weighted_p: You can spend more than one vote on a single item to signal how strongly you feel. A weighted vote counts as multiple votes from your budget but is still one voice.
about_voting_change_p: You can change or remove your vote at any time. The most recent vote is what counts.
about_fork_lead: When someone else's argument almost captures your view but needs adjustment, you fork it instead of writing a new one from scratch.
about_fork_when_p: You agree with the direction of an argument but disagree with the wording, the emphasis, or a specific claim. Forking keeps the lineage visible and lets others compare versions.
about_fork_step_1: Open the argument on a thesis page.
about_fork_step_2: Click the fork button and edit the text.
about_fork_step_3: Save. Your fork appears alongside the original, with a link back to the source.
about_fork_feed_p_html: If you voted on the original argument and someone forks it, the fork appears in the orange Fork updates panel in the left sidebar. You can keep your original vote or switch to the new version.
about_complexity_lead: One slider decides how much you see: fewer items when you want focus, more when you want breadth.
about_complexity_what_p: It caps the number of theses per view and the number of arguments per stance column on a thesis page. Everything filtered out is still there — it just isn't shown until you turn the slider up or reach the item another way.
about_complexity_effect_1_html: Thesis lists (home, categories, pulse) show up to the cap; the rest are scrollable but not front-and-centre.
about_complexity_effect_2_html: Thesis pages show the top N supporting and top N rejecting arguments; the remainder go into the pool below.
about_complexity_effect_3_html: Update columns (/my/updates) show the most recent N reactions per category.
about_complexity_tip_p: Start low, then raise the slider when you want the long tail. It's per-session, so it doesn't leak between people using the same device.
